text:
Two-timeOlympicchampion Dame Kelly Holmes has opened up about her action-packed life that saw the legendary runner enjoy a near-10-year career in the army before going on to reveal her sexuality. The 54-year-old announced that she was gay in 2022, ahead of her Kelly Holmes: Being Me documentary.

It was there that she discussed the realisation of her sexuality during her time serving in the Women's Royal Army Corps back in 1988. Kent-born Holmes said during an appearance on This Morning that she kept that side of her hidden as at the time it was still illegal for a gay person to join the army.

The law was only discarded in 2000 with Holmes knowing that to keep the job she loved, she'd have to conceal her true sexuality as she went on to describe the "humiliating" interrogations she endured on base.

Discussing the interrogations that saw her belongings dashed across the room, Holmes shared: "It was really difficult because there was interrogation that happened. In the documentary, I explain it a lot and I speak to people that dealt with some shocking things, but for me personally, having raids, it was scary, humiliating, embarrassing.

"People were tipped off, the Royal Military Police would come into your barracks and literally turn everything upside down, take everything out, you'd be left with your belongings lying around. They were trying to find any evidence you might be. If you got tipped off… you'd put everything into a box that wasn't anything to do with your sexuality, into a box and hide it in the boot of a car because you don't want to risk being court marshalled."

One year after the documentary, then-Prime ministerRishi Sunakapologised on behalf of the government for what Holmes and other gay soldiers had to go through with the former athlete responding to the apology on Loose Women, where she opened up about the toll her mental health took due to hiding her sexuality in the army.

"I was raided. The royal military police would come into my private space, my bedroom, and ransack it completely, be intimidating, interrogating, be in my face. I was scared. I was 23. I was petrified. I didn't want to lose my career. For me personally that caused me mental health issues throughout my whole career. I was so petrified as the shame was institutionally driven into me, hence why I didn't come out until last year."



It was only in June 2022 when the Pembury native publicly came out during an exclusive interview with theSunday Mirroras she expressed her delight in being able to announce the news on her own terms. "I needed to do this now, for me," she said. "It was my decision. I'm nervous about saying it. I feel like I'm going to explode with excitement. Sometimes I cry with relief. The moment this comes out, I'm essentially getting rid of that fear."

Holmes revealed the moment she realised she was gay after sharing a kiss with a fellow soldier at a bathroom block. "I realised I must be gay then because it felt good. It felt more natural, I felt comfortable," she recalled. Having admitted to being involved in secret relationships with other female soldiers, she was well aware of the consequences that would befall her should she have gotten caught.

"Everyone knew who was gay, but you’d never talk about it," she continued. "There was this pub that had a back dance floor and a pool table and everyone we knew was gay used to go to this place. You could be yourself, then come back to your barracks." It was in 1997 that Holmes left the army to pursue athletics as a full-time career and came out to her family, admitting that she used her athletic career to cope with her mental health, which plunged years later.



Holmes finally opened up about her current romance after a lifetime of having to keep them hidden with the Olympics legend in a relationship with girlfriend Louise Cullen, who she met during the pandemic and now lives with. The pair formed a motivational online fitness community and Cullen - who is originally from Northern Ireland - shares a love of sports with Holmes while running her own sports massage company.

During an interview with the SundayMirror, a loved-up Holmes said: "She loves me for me. I'm just happy. I'm happy with Lou. It makes it a lot easier being with somebody that I really get on with, and who just likes me for me. Lou's a different person, she's a nice person. She's somebody that doesn't want anything. She's not really interested in the work side of me, she doesn't really want to be in the limelight.

"And now she’s coming to England so let’s see what happens. Who knows what is around the corner. I’m not going to put pressure on myself and her and say this is forever – but hopefully it is."

title: Kelly Holmes now - Brit army role, moving in with girlfriend, olympics | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fasika Zelealem
description: Dame Kelly Holmes opened up about her sexuality struggle during her 10-year army career, which came before her legacy-making performances at the 2004 Olympics.
